title: Tocoyena cubensis
intro: Tocoyena cubensis, also known as Cuban rosewood, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its fragrant flowers and heartwood. Traditionally, Tocoyena cubensis has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tocoyena cubensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Tocoyena cubensis is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, often used to treat fever, colds, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Tocoyena cubensis is classified within the family Rubiaceae and belongs to the genus Tocoyena. It is native to Cuba and is a species of flowering plant. This species is part of the Rubiales order and is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which includes coffee and gardenia. Further classification places Tocoyena cubensis within the Rubieae tribe and the Tocoyeninae subtribe. Tocoyena cubensis is also classified as a member of the Gentianales clade.
